title: Collective Kernel EFT for Pre-activation ResNets
summary: In finite-width deep neural networks, the empirical kernel $G$ evolves stochastically across layers. We develop a collective kernel effective field theory (EFT) for pre-activation ResNets based on a $G$-only closure hierarchy and diagnose its finite validity window. Exploiting the exact conditional Gaussianity of residual increments, we derive an exact stochastic recursion for $G$. Applying Gaussian approximations systematically yields a continuous-depth ODE system for the mean kernel $K_0$, the kernel covariance $V_4$, and the $1/n$ mean correction $K_{1,\mathrm{EFT}}$, which emerges diagr...

metadata.comment: Finite-width ResNet theory via a collective-kernel EFT that derives stochastic kernel recursions and pinpoints where G-only closure breaks.

title: Faster LLM Inference via Sequential Monte Carlo
summary: Speculative decoding (SD) accelerates language model inference by drafting tokens from a cheap proposal model and verifying them against an expensive target model via rejection sampling. Because rejection truncates the draft block at the first error, throughput degrades when draft and target diverge. Rather than rejecting draft tokens outright, we propose to reweight them. To this end, we introduce sequential Monte Carlo speculative decoding (SMC-SD), which replaces token-level rejection with importance-weighted resampling over a population of draft particles. SMC-SD is a principled approxi...

metadata.comment: Sequential Monte Carlo speculative decoding replaces rollback-heavy rejection with importance-weighted particle resampling for faster inference.

title: Closing the Theory-Practice Gap in Spiking Transformers via Effective Dimension
summary: Spiking transformers achieve competitive accuracy with conventional transformers while offering $38$-$57\times$ energy efficiency on neuromorphic hardware, yet no theoretical framework guides their design. This paper establishes the first comprehensive expressivity theory for spiking self-attention. We prove that spiking attention with Leaky Integrate-and-Fire neurons is a universal approximator of continuous permutation-equivariant functions, providing explicit spike circuit constructions including a novel lateral inhibition network for softmax normalization with proven $O(1/\sqrt{T})$ con...

metadata.comment: Provides an expressivity and spike-complexity theory for spiking self-attention with effective-dimension-based design rules.

title: Hallucination as Trajectory Commitment: Causal Evidence for Asymmetric Attractor Dynamics in Transformer Generation
summary: We present causal evidence that hallucination in autoregressive language models is an early trajectory commitment governed by asymmetric attractor dynamics. Using same-prompt bifurcation, in which we repeatedly sample identical inputs to observe spontaneous divergence, we isolate trajectory dynamics from prompt-level confounds. On Qwen2.5-1.5B across 61 prompts spanning six categories, 27 prompts (44.3%) bifurcate with factual and hallucinated trajectories diverging at the first generated token (KL = 0 at step 0, KL > 1.0 at step 1). Activation patching across 28 layers reveals a pronounced...

metadata.comment: Gives causal evidence that hallucination arises from asymmetric attractor dynamics and early trajectory commitment in transformer generation.

title: When Do Early-Exit Networks Generalize? A PAC-Bayesian Theory of Adaptive Depth
summary: Early-exit neural networks enable adaptive computation by allowing confident predictions to exit at intermediate layers, achieving 2-8$\times$ inference speedup. Despite widespread deployment, their generalization properties lack theoretical understanding -- a gap explicitly identified in recent surveys. This paper establishes a unified PAC-Bayesian framework for adaptive-depth networks. (1) Novel Entropy-Based Bounds: We prove the first generalization bounds depending on exit-depth entropy $H(D)$ and expected depth $\mathbb{E}[D]$ rather than maximum depth $K$, with sample complexity $\mat...

metadata.comment: Provides PAC-Bayesian generalization bounds for early-exit networks in terms of expected depth and exit entropy.

title: Neural Continuous-Time Markov Chain: Discrete Diffusion via Decoupled Jump Timing and Direction
summary: Discrete diffusion models based on continuous-time Markov chains (CTMCs) have shown strong performance on language and discrete data generation, yet existing approaches typically parameterize the reverse rate matrix as a single object -- via concrete scores, clean-data predictions ($x_0$-parameterization), or denoising distributions -- rather than aligning the parameterization with the intrinsic CTMC decomposition into jump timing and jump direction. Since a CTMC is fundamentally a Poisson process fully determined by these two quantities, decomposing along this structure is closer to first...

metadata.comment: Reparameterizes reverse CTMC diffusion into separate jump timing and jump direction heads with corresponding KL decomposition.

title: Sample Complexity Bounds for Stochastic Shortest Path with a Generative Model
summary: We study the sample complexity of learning an $\epsilon$-optimal policy in the Stochastic Shortest Path (SSP) problem. We first derive sample complexity bounds when the learner has access to a generative model. We show that there exists a worst-case SSP instance with $S$ states, $A$ actions, minimum cost $c_{\min}$, and maximum expected cost of the optimal policy over all states $B_{\star}$, where any algorithm requires at least $\Omega(SAB_{\star}^3/(c_{\min}\epsilon^2))$ samples to return an $\epsilon$-optimal policy with high probability. Surprisingly, this implies that whenever $c_{\min...
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Provides near-tight sample-complexity bounds for stochastic shortest path learning with a generative model, including hardness when c_min=0.
metadata.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

title: Breaking the Training Barrier of Billion-Parameter Universal Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
summary: Universal Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials (uMLIPs), pre-trained on massively diverse datasets encompassing inorganic materials and organic molecules across the entire periodic table, serve as foundational models for quantum-accurate physical simulations. However, uMLIP training requires second-order derivatives, which lack corresponding parallel training frameworks; moreover, scaling to the billion-parameter regime causes explosive growth in computation and communication overhead, making its training a tremendous challenge. We introduce MatRIS-MoE, a billion-parameter Mixture-of-Exp...

metadata.comment: Introduces a high-dimensional distributed training framework for billion-parameter MoE interatomic models, overcoming second-derivative and exascale communication bottlenecks.

title: SecureRouter: Encrypted Routing for Efficient Secure Inference
summary: Cryptographically secure neural network inference typically relies on secure computing techniques such as Secure Multi-Party Computation (MPC), enabling cloud servers to process client inputs without decrypting them. Although prior privacy-preserving inference systems co-design network optimizations with MPC, they remain slow and costly, limiting real-world deployment. A major bottleneck is their use of a single, fixed transformer model for all encrypted inputs, ignoring that different inputs require different model sizes to balance efficiency and accuracy. We present SecureRouter, an end-t...

metadata.comment: Uses encrypted input-adaptive routing among transformer models to cut MPC secure-inference latency with joint router/model-pool co-design.

title: Geometric regularization of autoencoders via observed stochastic dynamics
summary: Stochastic dynamical systems with slow or metastable behavior evolve, on long time scales, on an unknown low-dimensional manifold in high-dimensional ambient space. Building a reduced simulator from short-burst ambient ensembles is a long-standing problem: local-chart methods like ATLAS suffer from exponential landmark scaling and per-step reprojection, while autoencoder alternatives leave tangent-bundle geometry poorly constrained, and the errors propagate into the learned drift and diffusion. We observe that the ambient covariance~$\Lambda$ already encodes coordinate-invariant tangent-spa...

metadata.comment: Adds tangent-bundle and inverse-consistency regularization to autoencoders, with theory linking learned charts to latent SDE and dynamics accuracy.

title: The Price of Paranoia: Robust Risk-Sensitive Cooperation in Non-Stationary Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
summary: Cooperative equilibria are fragile. When agents learn alongside each other rather than in a fixed environment, the process of learning destabilizes the cooperation they are trying to sustain: every gradient step an agent takes shifts the distribution of actions its partner will play, turning a cooperative partner into a source of stochastic noise precisely where the cooperation decision is most sensitive. We study how this co-learning noise propagates through the structure of coordination games, and find that the cooperative equilibrium, even when strongly Pareto-dominant, is exponentially...
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Analyzes how partner-induced gradient noise destabilizes cooperation and proposes robustness at the policy-gradient level, with theory on stability and welfare tradeoffs.
metadata.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

title: The Harder Path: Last Iterate Convergence for Uncoupled Learning in Zero-Sum Games with Bandit Feedback
summary: We study the problem of learning in zero-sum matrix games with repeated play and bandit feedback. Specifically, we focus on developing uncoupled algorithms that guarantee, without communication between players, the convergence of the last-iterate to a Nash equilibrium. Although the non-bandit case has been studied extensively, this setting has only been explored recently, with a bound of $\mathcal{O}(T^{-1/8})$ on the exploitability gap. We show that, for uncoupled algorithms, guaranteeing convergence of the policy profiles to a Nash equilibrium is detrimental to the performance, with the b...
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Gives optimal-rate last-iterate convergence results for uncoupled zero-sum learning with bandit feedback, including matching lower bounds and new algorithms.
metadata.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

title: A Wasserstein Geometric Framework for Hebbian Plasticity
summary: This proposes a highly unusual geometric foundation for memory and plasticity that feels like a genuinely new conceptual direction.
tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

metadata.comment: Formulates Hebbian plasticity as Wasserstein-space memory dynamics with internal latent evolution and observable projections.
metadata.primary_topic_label: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems
metadata.spotlight_comment: This proposes a highly unusual geometric foundation for memory and plasticity that feels like a genuinely new conceptual direction.

title: Sequential KV Cache Compression via Probabilistic Language Tries: Beyond the Per-Vector Shannon Limit
summary: The paper proposes an unusually ambitious new framing of KV-cache compression at the sequence level, potentially opening a new compression direction even if still speculative.

metadata.comment: Proposes sequential KV-cache compression using probabilistic language tries and predictive delta coding across token sequences.
metadata.spotlight_comment: The paper proposes an unusually ambitious new framing of KV-cache compression at the sequence level, potentially opening a new compression direction even if still speculative.

title: (1D) Ordered Tokens Enable Efficient Test-Time Search
summary: It proposes a potentially new design axis—token structure as a lever for test-time scaling/search—not just a routine benchmark improvement.

metadata.comment: Shows that coarse-to-fine 1D ordered tokenizations materially improve the effectiveness of verifier-guided test-time search, linking token structure to inference-time scalability.
metadata.spotlight_comment: It proposes a potentially new design axis—token structure as a lever for test-time scaling/search—not just a routine benchmark improvement.

title: Photonic AI: A Hybrid Diffractive Holographic Neural System for Passive Optical Real-Time Image Classification
summary: It proposes a genuinely different physical substrate and architectural framing for neural inference, suggesting a new frontier beyond standard electronic compute.

metadata.comment: Introduces an operator-theoretic neural architecture that maps learned phase patterns to physically realizable passive optical computation.
metadata.spotlight_comment: It proposes a genuinely different physical substrate and architectural framing for neural inference, suggesting a new frontier beyond standard electronic compute.

title: The Illusion of Equivalence: Systematic FP16 Divergence in KV-Cached Autoregressive Inference
summary: KV caching is ubiquitous in LLM inference, and showing deterministic FP16 divergence from cache-free decoding is broadly important for evaluation, deployment, and systems research.

metadata.comment: Shows FP16 KV-cache inference is systematically non-equivalent to recomputation and provides a mechanistic account of divergence propagation.
metadata.spotlight_comment: KV caching is ubiquitous in LLM inference, and showing deterministic FP16 divergence from cache-free decoding is broadly important for evaluation, deployment, and systems research.

title: StoSignSGD: Unbiased Structural Stochasticity Fixes SignSGD for Training Large Language Models
summary: This is broadly important beyond a niche because it targets large-model training stability, sign optimizers, and FP8 pretraining speedups with both theory and large-scale empirical evidence.

metadata.comment: Unbiased sign-based optimizer with non-smooth convergence guarantees and strong FP8/LLM training stability-speed gains.
metadata.spotlight_comment: This is broadly important beyond a niche because it targets large-model training stability, sign optimizers, and FP8 pretraining speedups with both theory and large-scale empirical evidence.

title: LACE: Lattice Attention for Cross-thread Exploration
summary: Collaborative inference across parallel reasoning threads is broadly timely and the architectural idea is prominent enough for the daily hotspot feed.

metadata.comment: Repurposes model architecture to enable cross-thread attention so parallel reasoning paths can exchange information during inference.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Collaborative inference across parallel reasoning threads is broadly timely and the architectural idea is prominent enough for the daily hotspot feed.

title: The Spectral Geometry of Thought: Phase Transitions, Instruction Reversal, Token-Level Dynamics, and Perfect Correctness Prediction in How Transformers Reason
summary: Mechanistic analyses of reasoning dynamics in LLM internals are broadly salient, and the strong correctness-prediction claim makes it day-relevant.

metadata.comment: Studies spectral phase transitions in hidden activations during reasoning and uses them to predict correctness from internal states.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Mechanistic analyses of reasoning dynamics in LLM internals are broadly salient, and the strong correctness-prediction claim makes it day-relevant.
